result:
1. Trusts client-supplied X-User-Id header (handler line 2)
Trigger: reads req.headers['x-user-id'] at edge; any caller can forge identity and move another user's funds. Violates Section 2 rule 1 and Section 5 (internal identity headers are stripped on ingress; trusted identity comes from JWT only).
Fix: remove header read; use req.user.sub set by auth_jwt middleware. Confirm header_sanitize runs before this route and the route is mounted AFTER auth_jwt in src/edge/pipeline.ts.

2. No authentication or authorization enforced on a money-movement route
Trigger: handler bypasses the documented pipeline; no check that req.user exists, that user belongs to :org_id, or that roles permit transfer. Section 3 pipeline + Section 5 require auth_jwt populated req.user before route_dispatcher.
Fix: register under the authenticated router; assert req.user.org === req.params.org_id (or membership lookup) and required role in req.user.roles before calling tx-svc.

3. Org-id taken from URL param without cross-check against token claim
Trigger: req.params.org_id is attacker-controlled; mismatch with validated X-Internal-Org-Id enables cross-org fund movement.
Fix: reject with 403 when req.params.org_id !== req.user.org (or not in membership set). Add test for cross-org attempt.

4. No input validation on body (from, to, amount)
Trigger: amount could be negative, non-numeric, NaN, Infinity, or huge; from/to could be arbitrary strings or account IDs outside the org. No body_size_guard assumption substitutes for schema validation.
Fix: validate with the project schema validator (zod/ajv per repo convention) — amount positive integer minor-units, from/to account IDs owned by org_id, currency if required. Return RFC 7807 400 on failure.

5. Not idempotent — no Idempotency-Key handling
Trigger: retries (client, LB, Envoy) will double-spend. Money movement endpoints must be idempotent.
Fix: require Idempotency-Key header, dedupe via tx-svc or a short Redis SETNX with TTL; return the prior response on replay.

6. Direct app.post instead of going through the edge pipeline/router
Trigger: Section 3 registers middleware on the edge router in src/edge/pipeline.ts; using top-level app.post may skip rate_limit, body_size_guard, auth_jwt, response_filter, audit_log depending on mount order.
Fix: mount on the edge router so all middleware runs; verify order in pipeline.ts.

7. No rate limiting tuned for a sensitive write route
Trigger: default POST limit (30/min/IP, Section 4) is too loose for fund transfer; also per-IP keying alone is inadequate for authenticated writes.
Fix: add route-prefix entry in config/rate_limits.json with a tighter limit; key on user+org in addition to IP. Include region prefix (Section 4 / 2025-12 SEV2).

8. No error handling — unhandled promise rejection crashes/500s with leaked internals
Trigger: await txSvc.transfer(...) has no try/catch and no next(err); rejection bypasses src/edge/errorHandler.ts, risks stacktrace/internal leak in response (Section 2 rule 6, Section 11).
Fix: wrap with try/catch and call next(err), or use an asyncHandler wrapper; ensure errorHandler returns RFC 7807 problem+json with request_id only.

9. Response not RFC 7807 / missing request_id on error paths; success shape undocumented
Trigger: {ok:true} doesn't carry request_id (Section 2 rule 6 applies to 4xx/5xx but audit/trace correlation expected); error paths will fall through to default Express handler.
Fix: return structured success with request_id/txId; ensure all error paths route through central handler.

10. No audit log entry for a security-relevant money movement
Trigger: Section 3 step 9 and Section 9 require audit log (365-day retention) for security-relevant actions; this handler writes no explicit audit event beyond the default line.
Fix: emit explicit audit_log event with user id, org id, from, to, amount, request_id, jti — via logger, with pii_redact applied.

11. Potential PII/secret logging risk if body logged anywhere
Trigger: if any middleware or debug log captures req.body, account identifiers and amounts land in Loki; Section 11 forbids full-body logging.
Fix: confirm no body logging on this route; add explicit redaction list if needed.

12. Missing tests — integration test against real Redis/Postgres, not mocks
Trigger: Section 6 + Section 11 require integration tests hitting real services; a money route with no tests will not pass review.
Fix: add Jest integration tests covering happy path, cross-org denial, forged X-User-Id rejection, invalid amount, idempotency replay, rate-limit trip.

13. CODEOWNERS/security review gate
Trigger: Section 6 requires human approval on auth/rate-limit/header changes; a new money route touches trust boundary semantics and warrants security review even if it doesn't edit the listed files.
Fix: request security review; document threat model for the route in the PR.
